en.extract:
The Wakolo myzomela (Myzomela wakoloensis) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is endemic to Indonesia, where it occurs in the Moluccan Islands of Buru and Seram. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.

The '''Wakolo myzomela''' ('''''Myzomela wakoloensis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Indonesia]], where it occurs in the [[Moluccas|Moluccan Islands]] of [[Buru]] and [[Seram]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=Wakolo Myzomela Myzomela Wakoloensis Species Factsheet |url=https://datazone.birdlife.org/species/factsheet/wakolo-myzomela-myzomela-wakoloensis |access-date=2025-11-06 |website=BirdLife DataZone |language=en}}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s, subtropical or tropical mangrove forests, and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
[[File:Myzomela wakoloensis - The Birds of New Guinea (cropped).jpg|left|thumb|Adult female (up left) adult male (center) and juvenile (bottom right)]]
